p.sum: From sneaking out of bed to watch MS-DOS boot, to building wind turbines in Haiti and architecting software systems — the origin story of a developer shaped by curiosity and constraint.
A civic tech experiment in radical transparency — building a platform for citizens to vote on real legislative proposals and hold elected officials accountable.
First post on the blog — a blank canvas and a note about what's coming.
Thoughts on productivity when AI tools fail, debugging legacy stacks, and the importance of boilerplate over clever solutions.
Pivoting from burnout to building: launching a civic engagement platform and an open-source social media alternative while reflecting on what drives the work.
A new format to make websites AI-readable, reducing token consumption by 96% and cutting AI query costs — the llmr.json spec proposal.
A reflection on managing a heavy workload alone while my working buddy is out, and the lessons learned about delegation, focus, and knowing when to stop.
Being on too many frontlines makes you immobile — a reflection on priorities, expertise, and knowing when to say no, inspired by aikido principles.
When AI goes overboard: Claude creates an entire game project from a simple idea discussion, complete with assets, scripts, and documentation nobody asked for.
Foreign key nightmares, table names that lie, and validation bugs in a legacy Laravel codebase — debugging enterprise systems where the schema and the code disagree.
PowerPoint documentation, Drupal hooks firing too early, and web services returning different data than what's in the database — a war story from legacy enterprise Drupal.
How I used AI to debug collision, physics, and scripting issues to build a working 3D character controller in Godot.
Conflicting email systems, redirect loops, and AI-generated boilerplate in a legacy Drupal workflow — what went wrong and how it was fixed.
After using GPT for a while, I found that Claude's approach to coding tasks was more aligned with my workflow — a comparison of both tools in practice.
Online scams are everywhere. Here's the story of how a script kiddy tried to extort me for $200 in Bitcoin and failed spectacularly.
Summer is almost over — working on side projects with GPT and Claude, and what took 3 days to fail with one took a few hours to ship with the other.
Struggling with productivity while working on a house bidding website, smart mirror hardware projects, and client deliverables all at once.
Working on automatically updating the blog with git push — a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys on every commit.
This week was chaos and cleanup: migrating SVN to Git, troubleshooting Laravel deployment issues, and untangling CET webforms.
The web should be simple. Frameworks bloat, obfuscate, and complicate things that don't need to be complicated — a case for building without them.

serv.d: Tech audits, team mentoring, roadmap planning
